llm_response: The fall of the Roman Empire was a complex process driven by centuries of internal decay and external pressure. Political instability weakened the central government, making it difficult to coordinate a unified response to threats. Economic decline further eroded the empire's ability to maintain its vast borders and support a large standing army. Military defeats against various groups, including the barbarian invasions, eventually led to the collapse of Roman authority in the West.
